summary: Iteration 109: Series Release & Poster Generation Bug Fixes - All 13 tests PASSED. Code review verified: 1) Poster generation uses number_of_images=1 (not n=1) in both series_pipeline.py and film_pipeline.py. 2) No b64_json references in poster code (images are raw bytes). 3) Series release has only ONE db.tv_series.update_one call. 4) Release response includes release_event, audience_comments, audience_rating fields.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fixes verified via code review and API tests. Key findings: 1) series_pipeline.py lines 191 and 861 use number_of_images=1 correctly. 2) film_pipeline.py lines 1593 and 2121 use number_of_images=1 correctly. 3) No b64_json references found in either file. 4) release_series function (lines 959-1143) has only ONE db.tv_series.update_one call at lines 1093-1108. 5) Response includes release_event, audience_comments, audience_rating. 6) Frontend SeriesTVPipeline.jsx correctly handles release response with cinematic reveal. NOTE: No series in 'ready_to_release' status available to test actual release endpoint, but code review confirms fixes are correct.

poster_generation_n_parameter.description: Changed n=1 to number_of_images=1 in generate_images calls
poster_generation_n_parameter.verification: grep confirmed number_of_images=1 at lines 191, 861 (series) and 1593, 2121 (film)

poster_generation_b64_json.description: Removed b64_json references, images are now raw bytes
poster_generation_b64_json.verification: grep confirmed no b64_json references in either file

series_release_double_update.description: Removed premature first DB update, now single consolidated update
series_release_double_update.verification: Code review confirmed only ONE db.tv_series.update_one call in release_series function

notes: All bug fixes verified through code review and API testing. The poster generation now uses the correct API parameters (number_of_images=1 instead of n=1, and images[0] instead of images[0].b64_json). The series release function now has a single consolidated database update instead of two separate updates, preventing status inconsistency. No series in 'ready_to_release' status were available to test the actual release flow, but the code review confirms the fixes are correctly implemented.
